description_long: This is the core of a red apple that has been almost entirely eaten, leaving only the central fibrous core and a small amount of flesh near the top and bottom. The remaining skin is a vibrant red with some yellowish undertones, especially near the top, and the exposed flesh is pale and slightly uneven from bite marks. The top of the core shows the remnants of the apple's stem and a small area of browning, indicating exposure to air. The bottom is slightly flattened, with a bit of red skin still visible. The surface texture is a mix of smooth, moist flesh and rougher, fibrous core. The object appears organic, with no artificial elements or attachments.
description: A nearly eaten red apple core with exposed pale flesh and fibrous center.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full height of the apple core, with the stem and most of the remaining flesh visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the red skin at the top and the uneven, bitten surface of the core.
description_view_2: Back view reveals the remaining red skin and the fibrous structure of the core.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focuses on the top with the stem and a more pronounced browning area.
attribute_visible_damage_or_blemishes: browning at top, uneven bite marks
